issues.description: The mountedRef guard smoke test can false-pass if the guard is removed. After unmount, React 18 generally ignores state updates on unmounted hooks, and this test only waits for onstop/stopPromise to finish plus checks onstopDone is defined, so a post-unmount setState/showNotification path would still pass.
issues.recommendation: Make the post-unmount side effect observable. For example, spy on setTimeout/showNotification side effects after releasePicker and assert no notification timer is scheduled after unmount; also assert showSaveFilePicker was called and onstop stayed pending until releasePicker.
issues.deferRationale: P3 test-hardening gap; the stronger settle tests still cover the permanent-pending regression path.

issues.description: The stated filter matrix is not fully exercised: owned/joined are only asserted for a non-super-admin session. The later status loop runs owned/joined as super-admin but only checks status, so a regression that treats super-admin owned/joined as global would pass.
issues.recommendation: Add exact-scope cases for super-admin owned and joined, expecting ownerId/admin and members.some.userId/admin respectively, or convert the filter tests to a table covering role x filter.
issues.deferRationale: Primary permission isolation for filter=all + regular user is covered; this is a narrower matrix completeness gap.

issues.description: The response allowlist assertion is partial. The fixture includes internal fields such as templateId and updatedAt, but the test only denies selected fields, so adding those extra fields to the response would still pass despite the current route mapping excluding them.
issues.recommendation: Assert the exact sorted response key set, or at minimum add negative checks for templateId and updatedAt alongside the existing sensitive/internal fields.
issues.deferRationale: The explicitly called out sensitive fields inviteCode/accessSecret/ownerId are covered; this is stricter allowlist hardening.
